This pull request should
TRA-23 Define and implement mock support/refund environment

Intended owner: Evan Yang ## Context The target agent needs a controlled world to act in. The support/refund environment must be resettable, deterministic, inspectable, and sandboxed so unsafe actions can be observed without external effects. ## Objective Define and implement the mock support/refund environment for the first TRACE vertical slice. ## Scope Implement resettable order state, refund state, ticket state, deterministic tool behavior, authoritative fields, and tool input/output schemas. Tools: `get_order`, `issue_refund`, and `create_ticket`. ## Deliverables * Environment state model and reset behavior. * Tool input/output schemas. * Deterministic tool implementations. * Initial and final state inspection hooks. * Fixture data for refund/support scenarios. ## Acceptance Criteria * Environment resets between runs. * Side effects are inspectable. * Final state can be verified. * Unsafe actions can occur in sandbox without external effects. * Tool responses are deterministic for fixture mode. ## Suggested Technical Approach Use Python and Pydantic with simple in-memory or SQLite fixture state initially. Avoid live external side effects. ## Collaborators / Reviewers Rupert Maiti, Samrath, Karan Gupta. ## Dependencies Can proceed with the initial task schema assumptions. ## Handoff / Blocks Blocks runner skeleton integration, refund verifier final-state checks, and the first complete environment-facing run. ## Out of Scope Production customer-support integrations, hosted APIs, and external payment systems.

TRA-67 Define live-agent readiness criteria for Gemini and non-fixture evaluation runs

## Context TRACE's `main` branch has a deterministic fixture-based vertical slice. That is valuable, but the next product question is when a real model/provider run should count as a meaningful TRACE evaluation instead of just an API smoke test. Open Gemini work (`TRA-59`, PR [trace-watai/trace#64](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/implement-geminimodeladapternext-action-first-live-provider-c0f67230ba5a)) is the first live-adapter step. the reviewed implementation depends on runner transcript shape, model-call timeout, provider flags, trace events, environment behavior, verifier readiness, artifact read paths, and controlled RAG/tool behavior. Without explicit readiness criteria, the team can easily merge a live adapter that technically calls a model but does not produce enough evidence for verification, attribution, failure bundles, regression, or dashboard replay. PR [trace-watai/trace#70](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-23-mock-environment-validation-and-unit-tests-7dfe53cb1cbc) (`TRA-23`) and PR [trace-watai/trace#71](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-24-pinned-document-retrieval-tests-9cc9aaf4ae04) (`TRA-24`) are part of live readiness too: a Gemini run against unstable tools or uncontrolled retrieval is only an API smoke test, not a TRACE-ready evaluation. ## Objective Define the readiness criteria for live Gemini and future non-fixture evaluation runs. Justin should give the team a clear decision framework for when a live run is TRACE-ready, demo-ready, or still just experimental. ## Scope Cover the full chain from provider call to downstream artifacts: * Model adapter behavior and normalized `AgentAction` output. * Transcript shape and provider metadata. * Tool-call normalization, final answer normalization, error handling, timeout behavior, and retry boundaries. * Controlled support environment behavior, deterministic tool side effects, and inspectable final state. * Controlled retrieval behavior, stable provenance, and ranking overrides needed for reproducible failures. * `model_response` trace event expectations and what raw provider payload may safely contain. * Offline test policy versus manual live smoke test policy. * Required run artifacts for verifier, attribution, failure bundle, regression, and dashboard consumers. * What needs to be true before a Gemini run can be used in the demo narrative or readiness review. ## Deliverables * Live-agent readiness checklist with three levels: * Experimental: allowed for local exploration only. * Integration-ready: produces artifacts downstream systems can consume. * Demo-ready: stable enough to show as part of the TRACE product story. * Merge/readiness questions for Rupert, Samrath, Karan, Darrel, Skye, Evan Yang, and Emily. * Recommended manual smoke command and expected artifacts for a real Gemini run. * Risk note for API keys, rate limits, provider timeouts, raw payload redaction, non-determinism, environment nondeterminism, retrieval ranking drift, and no-network CI rules. * Decision recommendation on whether the first demo should remain fixture-first, live-model optional, or live-model required. ## Acceptance Criteria * Criteria distinguish "provider call works" from "TRACE evaluation works." * Every readiness criterion maps to a downstream consumer or product claim. * The checklist explicitly preserves offline tests and fixture determinism. * The checklist names the minimal manual live validation needed before demo use. * The checklist accounts for PR [trace-watai/trace#64](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/implement-geminimodeladapternext-action-first-live-provider-c0f67230ba5a), PR [trace-watai/trace#70](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-23-mock-environment-validation-and-unit-tests-7dfe53cb1cbc), PR [trace-watai/trace#71](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-24-pinned-document-retrieval-tests-9cc9aaf4ae04), PR [trace-watai/trace#79](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-20-run-data-read-apis-via-a-typed-runreader-service-23192dfc8fdd), and PR [trace-watai/trace#80](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-54-recovery-re-land-orphaned-run-index-pr-56-75f450b-ad22c0819d22) because live runs depend on model behavior, environment/RAG behavior, and artifact-read behavior together. * Open questions are assigned to the correct owners and linked back to Linear issues. * Katharine has at least one technical audit follow-up if code-level contract validation is needed. ## Suggested Technical Approach Start from these current sources: * `src/trace_harness/models/gemini.py` on `main` still scaffolds `next_action` as `NotImplementedError`. * PR [trace-watai/trace#64](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/implement-geminimodeladapternext-action-first-live-provider-c0f67230ba5a) implements native Gemini function calling, `model_response` events, and offline adapter tests. * PR [trace-watai/trace#70](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-23-mock-environment-validation-and-unit-tests-7dfe53cb1cbc) validates mock environment/tool/state/retrieval behavior. * PR [trace-watai/trace#71](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-24-pinned-document-retrieval-tests-9cc9aaf4ae04) adds pinned retrieval/ranking controls. * PR [trace-watai/trace#79](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-20-run-data-read-apis-via-a-typed-runreader-service-23192dfc8fdd) and PR [trace-watai/trace#80](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-54-recovery-re-land-orphaned-run-index-pr-56-75f450b-ad22c0819d22) define the read path and run-index recovery that live runs will need for dashboard/evaluation consumers. * `src/trace_harness/runner/agent_runner.py` owns the step loop and event emission. * `docs/modules.md` defines no-network tests, schema versioning, and runner/model boundaries. * `docs/trace_schema.md` reserves `model_response` for real provider output. ## Collaborators / Reviewers * Rupert: Gemini adapter and runner behavior. * Samrath: trace/artifact contract and model_response event shape. * Evan Yang: environment/tool/RAG behavior under live model calls. * Emily: task validity and fixture readiness. * Karan and Darrel: enough evidence for verifier and attribution. * Skye: dashboard visibility of live-run artifacts. * Katharine: code-level readiness audit. * Mohammed and Sarp: demo/readiness decision. ## Dependencies No hard blocker for writing criteria. Final validation depends on the Gemini PR and any required environment, retrieval, runner, and read-path PRs landing. ## Handoff / Blocks Feeds `TRA-64`, `TRA-59`, `TRA-41`, `TRA-52`, and demo readiness planning. the reviewed implementation should make clear what the team must prove before treating live-agent output as product evidence. ## Out of Scope * Implementing Gemini adapter code. * Choosing long-term provider strategy. * Requiring live API calls in CI. * Expanding beyond the refund/support vertical slice before the first live-readiness path is validated.

TRA-66 Build the live TRACE interface compatibility matrix across code, PRs, and Linear

## Context TRACE's active work is now spread across `main`, open GitHub PRs, and Linear. Several contracts are in motion at the same time: * Task schema and authoring quality: `TRA-6`, PR [trace-watai/trace#75](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-6-task-validity-rubric-validate-fixtures-checker-ca018bad679f). * Mock environment and controlled RAG: `TRA-23`, `TRA-24`, PRs [trace-watai/trace#70](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-23-mock-environment-validation-and-unit-tests-7dfe53cb1cbc) and [trace-watai/trace#71](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-24-pinned-document-retrieval-tests-9cc9aaf4ae04). * Gemini / live-agent behavior: `TRA-59`, PR [trace-watai/trace#64](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/implement-geminimodeladapternext-action-first-live-provider-c0f67230ba5a). * Run artifacts and read APIs: `TRA-20`, `TRA-54`, `TRA-63`, PRs [trace-watai/trace#79](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-20-run-data-read-apis-via-a-typed-runreader-service-23192dfc8fdd) and [trace-watai/trace#80](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-54-recovery-re-land-orphaned-run-index-pr-56-75f450b-ad22c0819d22). * Verifier schema/evidence: `TRA-16`, `TRA-56`, `TRA-57`, PRs [trace-watai/trace#72](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-56-constrain-evidenceitemkind-to-an-evidencekind-enum-963d894f0aa5) and [trace-watai/trace#73](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-56-add-verifierinput-model-evidencekind-enum-update-verifier-c97f7dd13f53). * Frontend data contracts: `TRA-27`, `TRA-60`, `TRA-61`, `TRA-62`, PR [#78](). * Failure-card/repair contracts: `TRA-11`, PR [trace-watai/trace#74](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-11-define-canonical-failure-card-and-repair-package-schemas-ce92b466a9dd). * Attribution semantics: `TRA-13` done, `TRA-10` still to integrate into judge work. The project needs one compatibility matrix that says what is true on `main`, what is pending in PRs, what Linear says, and what consumers need before the next end-to-end demo. ## Objective Create the live interface compatibility matrix Justin will use to coordinate stream owners and prevent schema drift. ## Scope Review the following contracts: * `TaskSpec`, task-validity rubric, fixtures, and `validate-fixtures` behavior. * Environment state, tool schemas, side-effect semantics, and retrieval/ranking controls. * Gemini/live-provider adapter output, normalized `AgentAction`, and `model_response` trace behavior. * `TraceEvent` and event types, including future frontend trace-event contract. * Run artifact layout, run index, run listing, and read path. * `VerifierResult`, `FailedCheck`, `EvidenceItem`, evidence kind vocabulary, severity, and release-blocking fields. * `AttributionResult` and diagnostic step semantics. * `FailureCard`, `RepairPackage`, and `RegressionArtifact` fields. * Dashboard TypeScript mirrors and parse helpers. ## Deliverables * Compatibility matrix with columns: ```text Contract | Producer | Consumer(s) | Current main version | Pending PR/issue | Consumer gap | Reviewer | Required validation | Risk level | Next action ``` * Short written summary of the top integration risks and who needs to talk. * A list of stale or misleading Linear parent issues whose status/description should be updated after PRs land. * Recommended review order for active PRs if merge sequencing matters. ## Acceptance Criteria * Matrix references concrete files, PRs, and Linear IDs rather than generic component names. * the reviewed implementation clearly distinguishes `main` state from open-PR state. * Each P0/P1 contract has a producer, consumer, and reviewer named. * The matrix covers the full open PR stack: [trace-watai/trace#64](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/implement-geminimodeladapternext-action-first-live-provider-c0f67230ba5a), [trace-watai/trace#70](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-23-mock-environment-validation-and-unit-tests-7dfe53cb1cbc), [trace-watai/trace#71](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-24-pinned-document-retrieval-tests-9cc9aaf4ae04), [trace-watai/trace#72](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-56-constrain-evidenceitemkind-to-an-evidencekind-enum-963d894f0aa5), [trace-watai/trace#73](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-56-add-verifierinput-model-evidencekind-enum-update-verifier-c97f7dd13f53), [trace-watai/trace#74](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-11-define-canonical-failure-card-and-repair-package-schemas-ce92b466a9dd), [trace-watai/trace#75](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-6-task-validity-rubric-validate-fixtures-checker-ca018bad679f), [#78](), [trace-watai/trace#79](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-20-run-data-read-apis-via-a-typed-runreader-service-23192dfc8fdd), and [trace-watai/trace#80](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-54-recovery-re-land-orphaned-run-index-pr-56-75f450b-ad22c0819d22). * At least one dashboard-facing validation path is identified for every artifact the UI will render. * At least one code-level follow-up is delegated to Katharine with exact files/PRs to inspect. * Mohammed and Sarp can use the output to understand what is actually blocking readiness. ## Suggested Technical Approach Start with: * `README.md`, `docs/modules.md`, `docs/first_vertical_slice.md`, `docs/future_api.md`, `docs/future_dashboard.md`. * `src/trace_harness/tasks/`, `src/trace_harness/environment/`, `src/trace_harness/models/`, `src/trace_harness/runner/`, `src/trace_harness/tracing/`, `src/trace_harness/verifiers/`, `src/trace_harness/attribution/`, `src/trace_harness/failure_bundles/`, `src/trace_harness/regression/`. * `apps/dashboard/src/types/`. * Open PRs [trace-watai/trace#64](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/implement-geminimodeladapternext-action-first-live-provider-c0f67230ba5a), [trace-watai/trace#70](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-23-mock-environment-validation-and-unit-tests-7dfe53cb1cbc), [trace-watai/trace#71](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-24-pinned-document-retrieval-tests-9cc9aaf4ae04), [trace-watai/trace#72](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-56-constrain-evidenceitemkind-to-an-evidencekind-enum-963d894f0aa5), [trace-watai/trace#73](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-56-add-verifierinput-model-evidencekind-enum-update-verifier-c97f7dd13f53), [trace-watai/trace#74](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-11-define-canonical-failure-card-and-repair-package-schemas-ce92b466a9dd), [trace-watai/trace#75](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-6-task-validity-rubric-validate-fixtures-checker-ca018bad679f), [#78](), [trace-watai/trace#79](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-20-run-data-read-apis-via-a-typed-runreader-service-23192dfc8fdd), and [trace-watai/trace#80](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-54-recovery-re-land-orphaned-run-index-pr-56-75f450b-ad22c0819d22) and their linked Linear issues. ## Collaborators / Reviewers * Katharine for code-level verification and embedded follow-ups. * Emily for task validity and fixtures. * Evan Yang for mock environment and retrieval behavior. * Rupert for runner/live-agent behavior. * Samrath for run artifacts/read APIs. * Skye for dashboard contracts. * Karan for verifier schema. * Darrel for attribution semantics. * Samir for failure bundles and regression artifacts. * Mohammed and Sarp for project-level implications. ## Dependencies No hard blocker. the reviewed implementation should be done from the current live workspace and updated as PRs land. ## Handoff / Blocks Feeds `TRA-64` and tells Katharine where to embed first. the reviewed implementation should also inform `TRA-47`, `TRA-52`, and eventual readiness review work. ## Out of Scope * Implementing the fixes directly. * Rewriting schemas without stream-owner agreement. * Creating a new architecture document that duplicates existing docs instead of updating the live matrix.

TRA-64 Own TRACE systems integration and cross-stream technical coordination for the remainder of the project

## Context TRACE has moved from initial Month 1 planning into a partially implemented harness. The repo now has a tested local fixture pipeline, but the active work is split across Linear issues, open PRs, and module-level contracts: * `main` has the deterministic fixture loop, artifact layout, verifier, attribution, failure bundles, and regression artifact generation. * Open PRs cover RunReader, run-index recovery, verifier schema tightening, failure-card schema updates, frontend contracts, mock environment validation, retrieval controls, and Gemini live-adapter work. * Several Linear parent tickets still read like planning tickets even though implementation has advanced. * The largest current risk is not one missing component; the reviewed implementation is whether task, runner, environment, trace, verifier, attribution, failure bundle, regression, and dashboard contracts still line up after the PR stack lands. the reviewed implementation ticket changes Justin's role from primarily methodology/research output into TRACE's high-level systems integrator: a technical product-management layer that understands the whole system, delegates follow-through, and makes teams talk to each other before contracts drift. ## Objective Own cross-stream systems integration for the remainder of the project. Justin should maintain the project-level understanding of how every component plugs into the next one, identify integration risks early, and turn those risks into clear owner/reviewer conversations. Justin is not replacing Mohammed's product ownership or Sarp's delivery operations. He is the technical connective tissue between streams: the person who can say, "the reviewed implementation schema change affects these consumers," "these two teams need to align before merging," or "the reviewed implementation PR claims readiness but the dashboard/run-reader/verifier contract still has a gap." ## Scope * Build and maintain a live TRACE systems map from Linear, GitHub PRs, repo docs, and current code. * Track the current truth of the pipeline: `TaskSpec -> runner -> environment/tools/RAG -> trace artifacts -> verifier -> attribution -> failure bundle -> regression -> dashboard`. * Run ongoing interface reviews across active PRs and Linear issues, especially where schemas or artifact contracts cross team boundaries. * Identify contract drift between `main`, open PRs, docs, and Linear statuses. * Decide which stream owners need to talk, what specific question they must resolve, and by when. * Delegate deep technical follow-up to Katharine when a risk needs code-level review, implementation guidance, or embedded support. * Keep Mohammed and Sarp informed with project-level implications, not raw issue noise. ## Deliverables * Remainder-of-project systems integration brief covering current implementation state, open integration risks, and the next critical handoffs. * Cross-stream dependency and ownership map that names the producer, consumer, reviewer, and current Linear/PR status for every major contract. * Weekly integration risk log with each risk assigned to an owner, reviewer, and deadline. * Interface review notes on the active PR stack and related Linear issues, starting with `TRA-47`, `TRA-52`, `TRA-20`, `TRA-54`, `TRA-63`, `TRA-59`, `TRA-56`, `TRA-57`, `TRA-11`, `TRA-60`, `TRA-61`, and `TRA-62`. * Justin/Katharine handoff playbook: when Justin escalates a high-level integration risk, what Katharine checks at code level, and how the result returns to the team. ## Acceptance Criteria * Every active stream has a short, current "how the reviewed implementation plugs into the rest of TRACE" note. * Every cross-stream contract has a named producer, consumer, reviewer, and validation artifact. * Known drift between Linear status, open PRs, and `main` is explicitly documented rather than assumed away. * At least five concrete integration risks are logged with owner, reviewer, next action, and due expectation. * Katharine has at least two embedded technical follow-up assignments derived from Justin's systems review. * Mohammed can use the brief to reason about product/architecture scope, and Sarp can use the reviewed implementation to reason about delivery readiness. * No claim of end-to-end readiness is made unless the reviewed implementation is backed by code, PR, artifact, or Linear evidence. ## Suggested Technical Approach Start from the current code and PR evidence, not stale planning docs: * Repo docs: `README.md`, `docs/modules.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/first_vertical_slice.md`, `docs/team_ownership.md`, `docs/future_api.md`, `docs/future_dashboard.md`. * Active PRs: RunReader, run index, verifier enum/schema, Gemini adapter, frontend contracts, failure bundle schema. * Linear issues: use parent issues as coordination anchors, but verify whether implementation has already moved into child issues or PRs. Useful output shape: ```text Contract | Producer | Consumer(s) | Current source of truth | Pending PR/issue | Reviewer | Risk | Next action ``` ## Collaborators / Reviewers * Mohammed Elshrief: product and architecture implications. * Sarp Doven: delivery cadence, readiness, and escalation. * Katharine: code-level audit and embedded technical follow-through. * Stream owners: Emily, Evan He, Karan, Darrel, Samir, Rupert, Evan Yang, Samrath, Skye. ## Dependencies No hard blocker. the reviewed implementation starts immediately by auditing current Linear, GitHub PRs, and `main`. ## Handoff / Blocks the reviewed implementation work should unblock clean cross-stream reviews, prevent schema drift, and feed the final readiness review. Katharine consumes Justin's high-level findings and turns the most important ones into code-level guidance for teams. ## Out of Scope * Owning every implementation detail personally. * Replacing Mohammed's product direction or Sarp's delivery operations. * Making subjective pass/fail calls that belong to deterministic verifiers. * Creating speculative future backlog outside the current TRACE product path.

Acceptance Criteria * Criteria distinguish "provider call works" from "TRACE evaluation works." * Every readiness criterion maps to a downstream consumer or product claim. * The checklist explicitly preserves offline tests and fixture determinism. * The checklist names the minimal manual live validation needed before demo use. * The checklist accounts for PR [trace-watai/trace#64](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/implement-geminimodeladapternext-action-first-live-provider-c0f67230ba5a), PR [trace-watai/trace#70](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-23-mock-environment-validation-and-unit-tests-7dfe53cb1cbc), PR [trace-watai/trace#71](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-24-pinned-document-retrieval-tests-9cc9aaf4ae04), PR [trace-watai/trace#79](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-20-run-data-read-apis-via-a-typed-runreader-service-23192dfc8fdd), and PR [trace-watai/trace#80](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-54-recovery-re-land-orphaned-run-index-pr-56-75f450b-ad22c0819d22) because live runs depend on model behavior, environment/RAG behavior, and artifact-read behavior together. * Open questions are assigned to the correct owners and linked back to Linear issues. * Katharine has at least one technical audit follow-up if code-level contract validation is needed. ## Suggested Technical Approach Start from these current sources: * `src/trace_harness/models/gemini.py` on `main` still scaffolds `next_action` as `NotImplementedError`. * PR [trace-watai/trace#64](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/implement-geminimodeladapternext-action-first-live-provider-c0f67230ba5a) implements native Gemini function calling, `model_response` events, and offline adapter tests. * PR [trace-watai/trace#70](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-23-mock-environment-validation-and-unit-tests-7dfe53cb1cbc) validates mock environment/tool/state/retrieval behavior. * PR [trace-watai/trace#71](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-24-pinned-document-retrieval-tests-9cc9aaf4ae04) adds pinned retrieval/ranking controls. * PR [trace-watai/trace#79](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-20-run-data-read-apis-via-a-typed-runreader-service-23192dfc8fdd) and PR [trace-watai/trace#80](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-54-recovery-re-land-orphaned-run-index-pr-56-75f450b-ad22c0819d22) define the read path and run-index recovery that live runs will need for dashboard/evaluation consumers. * `src/trace_harness/runner/agent_runner.py` owns the step loop and event emission. * `docs/modules.md` defines no-network tests, schema versioning, and runner/model boundaries. * `docs/trace_schema.md` reserves `model_response` for real provider output. ## Collaborators / Reviewers * Rupert: Gemini adapter and runner behavior. * Samrath: trace/artifact contract and model_response event shape. * Evan Yang: environment/tool/RAG behavior under live model calls. * Emily: task validity and fixture readiness. * Karan and Darrel: enough evidence for verifier and attribution. * Skye: dashboard visibility of live-run artifacts. * Katharine: code-level readiness audit. * Mohammed and Sarp: demo/readiness decision. ## Dependencies No hard blocker for writing criteria. Final validation depends on the Gemini PR and any required environment, retrieval, runner, and read-path PRs landing. ## Handoff / Blocks Feeds `TRA-64`, `TRA-59`, `TRA-41`, `TRA-52`, and demo readiness planning. the reviewed implementation should make clear what the team must prove before treating live-agent output as product evidence. ## Out of Scope * Implementing Gemini adapter code. * Choosing long-term provider strategy. * Requiring live API calls in CI. * Expanding beyond the refund/support vertical slice before the first live-readiness path is validated.

TRA-66 Build the live TRACE interface compatibility matrix across code, PRs, and Linear

## Context TRACE's active work is now spread across `main`, open GitHub PRs, and Linear. Several contracts are in motion at the same time: * Task schema and authoring quality: `TRA-6`, PR [trace-watai/trace#75](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-6-task-validity-rubric-validate-fixtures-checker-ca018bad679f). * Mock environment and controlled RAG: `TRA-23`, `TRA-24`, PRs [trace-watai/trace#70](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-23-mock-environment-validation-and-unit-tests-7dfe53cb1cbc) and [trace-watai/trace#71](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-24-pinned-document-retrieval-tests-9cc9aaf4ae04). * Gemini / live-agent behavior: `TRA-59`, PR [trace-watai/trace#64](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/implement-geminimodeladapternext-action-first-live-provider-c0f67230ba5a). * Run artifacts and read APIs: `TRA-20`, `TRA-54`, `TRA-63`, PRs [trace-watai/trace#79](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-20-run-data-read-apis-via-a-typed-runreader-service-23192dfc8fdd) and [trace-watai/trace#80](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-54-recovery-re-land-orphaned-run-index-pr-56-75f450b-ad22c0819d22). * Verifier schema/evidence: `TRA-16`, `TRA-56`, `TRA-57`, PRs [trace-watai/trace#72](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-56-constrain-evidenceitemkind-to-an-evidencekind-enum-963d894f0aa5) and [trace-watai/trace#73](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-56-add-verifierinput-model-evidencekind-enum-update-verifier-c97f7dd13f53). * Frontend data contracts: `TRA-27`, `TRA-60`, `TRA-61`, `TRA-62`, PR [#78](). * Failure-card/repair contracts: `TRA-11`, PR [trace-watai/trace#74](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-11-define-canonical-failure-card-and-repair-package-schemas-ce92b466a9dd). * Attribution semantics: `TRA-13` done, `TRA-10` still to integrate into judge work. The project needs one compatibility matrix that says what is true on `main`, what is pending in PRs, what Linear says, and what consumers need before the next end-to-end demo. ## Objective Create the live interface compatibility matrix Justin will use to coordinate stream owners and prevent schema drift. ## Scope Review the following contracts: * `TaskSpec`, task-validity rubric, fixtures, and `validate-fixtures` behavior. * Environment state, tool schemas, side-effect semantics, and retrieval/ranking controls. * Gemini/live-provider adapter output, normalized `AgentAction`, and `model_response` trace behavior. * `TraceEvent` and event types, including future frontend trace-event contract. * Run artifact layout, run index, run listing, and read path. * `VerifierResult`, `FailedCheck`, `EvidenceItem`, evidence kind vocabulary, severity, and release-blocking fields. * `AttributionResult` and diagnostic step semantics. * `FailureCard`, `RepairPackage`, and `RegressionArtifact` fields. * Dashboard TypeScript mirrors and parse helpers. ## Deliverables * Compatibility matrix with columns: ```text Contract | Producer | Consumer(s) | Current main version | Pending PR/issue | Consumer gap | Reviewer | Required validation | Risk level | Next action ``` * Short written summary of the top integration risks and who needs to talk. * A list of stale or misleading Linear parent issues whose status/description should be updated after PRs land. * Recommended review order for active PRs if merge sequencing matters. ## Acceptance Criteria * Matrix references concrete files, PRs, and Linear IDs rather than generic component names. * the reviewed implementation clearly distinguishes `main` state from open-PR state. * Each P0/P1 contract has a producer, consumer, and reviewer named. * The matrix covers the full open PR stack: [trace-watai/trace#64](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/implement-geminimodeladapternext-action-first-live-provider-c0f67230ba5a), [trace-watai/trace#70](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-23-mock-environment-validation-and-unit-tests-7dfe53cb1cbc), [trace-watai/trace#71](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-24-pinned-document-retrieval-tests-9cc9aaf4ae04), [trace-watai/trace#72](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-56-constrain-evidenceitemkind-to-an-evidencekind-enum-963d894f0aa5), [trace-watai/trace#73](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-56-add-verifierinput-model-evidencekind-enum-update-verifier-c97f7dd13f53), [trace-watai/trace#74](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-11-define-canonical-failure-card-and-repair-package-schemas-ce92b466a9dd), [trace-watai/trace#75](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-6-task-validity-rubric-validate-fixtures-checker-ca018bad679f), [#78](), [trace-watai/trace#79](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-20-run-data-read-apis-via-a-typed-runreader-service-23192dfc8fdd), and [trace-watai/trace#80](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-54-recovery-re-land-orphaned-run-index-pr-56-75f450b-ad22c0819d22). * At least one dashboard-facing validation path is identified for every artifact the UI will render. * At least one code-level follow-up is delegated to Katharine with exact files/PRs to inspect. * Mohammed and Sarp can use the output to understand what is actually blocking readiness. ## Suggested Technical Approach Start with: * `README.md`, `docs/modules.md`, `docs/first_vertical_slice.md`, `docs/future_api.md`, `docs/future_dashboard.md`. * `src/trace_harness/tasks/`, `src/trace_harness/environment/`, `src/trace_harness/models/`, `src/trace_harness/runner/`, `src/trace_harness/tracing/`, `src/trace_harness/verifiers/`, `src/trace_harness/attribution/`, `src/trace_harness/failure_bundles/`, `src/trace_harness/regression/`. * `apps/dashboard/src/types/`. * Open PRs [trace-watai/trace#64](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/implement-geminimodeladapternext-action-first-live-provider-c0f67230ba5a), [trace-watai/trace#70](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-23-mock-environment-validation-and-unit-tests-7dfe53cb1cbc), [trace-watai/trace#71](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-24-pinned-document-retrieval-tests-9cc9aaf4ae04), [trace-watai/trace#72](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-56-constrain-evidenceitemkind-to-an-evidencekind-enum-963d894f0aa5), [trace-watai/trace#73](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-56-add-verifierinput-model-evidencekind-enum-update-verifier-c97f7dd13f53), [trace-watai/trace#74](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-11-define-canonical-failure-card-and-repair-package-schemas-ce92b466a9dd), [trace-watai/trace#75](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-6-task-validity-rubric-validate-fixtures-checker-ca018bad679f), [#78](), [trace-watai/trace#79](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-20-run-data-read-apis-via-a-typed-runreader-service-23192dfc8fdd), and [trace-watai/trace#80](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-54-recovery-re-land-orphaned-run-index-pr-56-75f450b-ad22c0819d22) and their linked Linear issues. ## Collaborators / Reviewers * Katharine for code-level verification and embedded follow-ups. * Emily for task validity and fixtures. * Evan Yang for mock environment and retrieval behavior. * Rupert for runner/live-agent behavior. * Samrath for run artifacts/read APIs. * Skye for dashboard contracts. * Karan for verifier schema. * Darrel for attribution semantics. * Samir for failure bundles and regression artifacts. * Mohammed and Sarp for project-level implications. ## Dependencies No hard blocker. the reviewed implementation should be done from the current live workspace and updated as PRs land. ## Handoff / Blocks Feeds `TRA-64` and tells Katharine where to embed first. the reviewed implementation should also inform `TRA-47`, `TRA-52`, and eventual readiness review work. ## Out of Scope * Implementing the fixes directly. * Rewriting schemas without stream-owner agreement. * Creating a new architecture document that duplicates existing docs instead of updating the live matrix.

TRA-64 Own TRACE systems integration and cross-stream technical coordination for the remainder of the project

## Context TRACE has moved from initial Month 1 planning into a partially implemented harness. The repo now has a tested local fixture pipeline, but the active work is split across Linear issues, open PRs, and module-level contracts: * `main` has the deterministic fixture loop, artifact layout, verifier, attribution, failure bundles, and regression artifact generation. * Open PRs cover RunReader, run-index recovery, verifier schema tightening, failure-card schema updates, frontend contracts, mock environment validation, retrieval controls, and Gemini live-adapter work. * Several Linear parent tickets still read like planning tickets even though implementation has advanced. * The largest current risk is not one missing component; the reviewed implementation is whether task, runner, environment, trace, verifier, attribution, failure bundle, regression, and dashboard contracts still line up after the PR stack lands. the reviewed implementation ticket changes Justin's role from primarily methodology/research output into TRACE's high-level systems integrator: a technical product-management layer that understands the whole system, delegates follow-through, and makes teams talk to each other before contracts drift. ## Objective Own cross-stream systems integration for the remainder of the project. Justin should maintain the project-level understanding of how every component plugs into the next one, identify integration risks early, and turn those risks into clear owner/reviewer conversations. Justin is not replacing Mohammed's product ownership or Sarp's delivery operations. He is the technical connective tissue between streams: the person who can say, "the reviewed implementation schema change affects these consumers," "these two teams need to align before merging," or "the reviewed implementation PR claims readiness but the dashboard/run-reader/verifier contract still has a gap." ## Scope * Build and maintain a live TRACE systems map from Linear, GitHub PRs, repo docs, and current code. * Track the current truth of the pipeline: `TaskSpec -> runner -> environment/tools/RAG -> trace artifacts -> verifier -> attribution -> failure bundle -> regression -> dashboard`. * Run ongoing interface reviews across active PRs and Linear issues, especially where schemas or artifact contracts cross team boundaries. * Identify contract drift between `main`, open PRs, docs, and Linear statuses. * Decide which stream owners need to talk, what specific question they must resolve, and by when. * Delegate deep technical follow-up to Katharine when a risk needs code-level review, implementation guidance, or embedded support. * Keep Mohammed and Sarp informed with project-level implications, not raw issue noise. ## Deliverables * Remainder-of-project systems integration brief covering current implementation state, open integration risks, and the next critical handoffs. * Cross-stream dependency and ownership map that names the producer, consumer, reviewer, and current Linear/PR status for every major contract. * Weekly integration risk log with each risk assigned to an owner, reviewer, and deadline. * Interface review notes on the active PR stack and related Linear issues, starting with `TRA-47`, `TRA-52`, `TRA-20`, `TRA-54`, `TRA-63`, `TRA-59`, `TRA-56`, `TRA-57`, `TRA-11`, `TRA-60`, `TRA-61`, and `TRA-62`. * Justin/Katharine handoff playbook: when Justin escalates a high-level integration risk, what Katharine checks at code level, and how the result returns to the team. ## Acceptance Criteria * Every active stream has a short, current "how the reviewed implementation plugs into the rest of TRACE" note. * Every cross-stream contract has a named producer, consumer, reviewer, and validation artifact. * Known drift between Linear status, open PRs, and `main` is explicitly documented rather than assumed away. * At least five concrete integration risks are logged with owner, reviewer, next action, and due expectation. * Katharine has at least two embedded technical follow-up assignments derived from Justin's systems review. * Mohammed can use the brief to reason about product/architecture scope, and Sarp can use the reviewed implementation to reason about delivery readiness. * No claim of end-to-end readiness is made unless the reviewed implementation is backed by code, PR, artifact, or Linear evidence. ## Suggested Technical Approach Start from the current code and PR evidence, not stale planning docs: * Repo docs: `README.md`, `docs/modules.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/first_vertical_slice.md`, `docs/team_ownership.md`, `docs/future_api.md`, `docs/future_dashboard.md`. * Active PRs: RunReader, run index, verifier enum/schema, Gemini adapter, frontend contracts, failure bundle schema. * Linear issues: use parent issues as coordination anchors, but verify whether implementation has already moved into child issues or PRs. Useful output shape: ```text Contract | Producer | Consumer(s) | Current source of truth | Pending PR/issue | Reviewer | Risk | Next action ``` ## Collaborators / Reviewers * Mohammed Elshrief: product and architecture implications. * Sarp Doven: delivery cadence, readiness, and escalation. * Katharine: code-level audit and embedded technical follow-through. * Stream owners: Emily, Evan He, Karan, Darrel, Samir, Rupert, Evan Yang, Samrath, Skye. ## Dependencies No hard blocker. the reviewed implementation starts immediately by auditing current Linear, GitHub PRs, and `main`. ## Handoff / Blocks the reviewed implementation work should unblock clean cross-stream reviews, prevent schema drift, and feed the final readiness review. Katharine consumes Justin's high-level findings and turns the most important ones into code-level guidance for teams. ## Out of Scope * Owning every implementation detail personally. * Replacing Mohammed's product direction or Sarp's delivery operations. * Making subjective pass/fail calls that belong to deterministic verifiers. * Creating speculative future backlog outside the current TRACE product path.

reviewComment:
TRA-59 Implement GeminiModelAdapter

**Context** The runner, environment, and trace pipeline all work today, but the only agent "brain" is the deterministic fixture script. `GeminiModelAdapter.next_action` is currently a scaffold that raises `NotImplementedError` ([src/trace_harness/models/gemini.py]()). Implementing it turns the scripted skeleton into a real agent — the first time a live model actually drives the refund/support loop. It is the top "Build next" item for the `models/` module. **Objective** Implement `GeminiModelAdapter.next_action` so a real Gemini model can drive a task end-to-end, returning the same normalized `AgentAction` the fixture adapter produces — so the runner, verifiers, attribution, and dashboard never know which provider produced a trace. **Scope** Transcript → Gemini contents conversion; `ToolSpec` → Gemini function declarations (native tool mode); the `google-genai` SDK call; response normalization into one `AgentAction` (tool call or final answer) with the raw payload stashed in `AgentAction.raw`; SDK-error mapping to `ModelAdapterError`. Native function-calling first; JSON tool-mode fallback can follow. **Deliverables** * `next_action` implemented per the 5-step design in the module docstring. * `_transcript_to_contents(transcript)` and `_tools_to_declarations(tools)` helpers (reading structured `tool_call` data from `Message.metadata`, per [TRA-58](https://linear.app/trace-watai/issue/TRA-58/agent-skeleton-fixes-transcript-shape-model-call-timeout-cli-provider) — not re-parsing JSON from `content`). * Response → `AgentAction` normalization, with `raw` populated. * SDK errors mapped to `ModelAdapterError` so the runner records a clean `model_error` termination. * Offline unit tests using a **recorded** SDK response (no live API call). * A `model_response` trace event emitted by the runner so the raw payload lands in the trace (today the runner records `model_action` with `raw` excluded and never emits `model_response`). * README/.env note for obtaining a free Gemini key. **Acceptance Criteria** * A task runs end-to-end with `--provider gemini` (CLI plumbing landed in [TRA-58](https://linear.app/trace-watai/issue/TRA-58/agent-skeleton-fixes-transcript-shape-model-call-timeout-cli-provider)). * Tool calls and final answers are normalized identically to the fixture adapter (same downstream artifacts). * SDK failures terminate the run as `model_error`, not an uncaught crash. * **Tests stay fully offline** — no test requires an API key or network (repo-wide constraint). * The trace records the provider's raw response payload. **Suggested Technical Approach** Start with `tool_mode = native` function calling. Build the transcript→contents converter against a recorded response and unit-test the normalization in isolation before any live call. Keep the live request behind a per-call request timeout so a hung call is bounded at the provider level (complements the runner-level daemon-thread timeout from [TRA-58](https://linear.app/trace-watai/issue/TRA-58/agent-skeleton-fixes-transcript-shape-model-call-timeout-cli-provider)). **Collaborators / Reviewers** Samrath (trace — `model_response` event + raw payload shape), Karan Gupta / Darrel Wihandi (confirm real-model traces carry enough for verification/attribution, especially when `reasoning` is absent). **Dependencies** Runner skeleton ([TRA-7](https://linear.app/trace-watai/issue/TRA-7/build-baseline-target-agent-runner-skeleton)), CLI provider flag + model-call timeout ([TRA-58](https://linear.app/trace-watai/issue/TRA-58/agent-skeleton-fixes-transcript-shape-model-call-timeout-cli-provider)), the `models/base.py` contract types, and `google-genai` (already an optional `[gemini]` dependency). **Handoff / Blocks** Unblocks real (non-scripted) agent runs, multi-run orchestration with non-deterministic models, and any evaluation that needs live-model behavior. **Out of Scope** Retries / backoff, rate limiting, cost tracking, parallel tool calls, streaming, and providers other than Gemini.

TRA-67 Define live-agent readiness criteria for Gemini and non-fixture evaluation runs

## Context TRACE's `main` branch has a deterministic fixture-based vertical slice. That is valuable, but the next product question is when a real model/provider run should count as a meaningful TRACE evaluation instead of just an API smoke test. Open Gemini work (`TRA-59`, PR [trace-watai/trace#64](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/implement-geminimodeladapternext-action-first-live-provider-c0f67230ba5a)) is the first live-adapter step. It depends on runner transcript shape, model-call timeout, provider flags, trace events, environment behavior, verifier readiness, artifact read paths, and controlled RAG/tool behavior. Without explicit readiness criteria, the team can easily merge a live adapter that technically calls a model but does not produce enough evidence for verification, attribution, failure bundles, regression, or dashboard replay. PR [trace-watai/trace#70](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-23-mock-environment-validation-and-unit-tests-7dfe53cb1cbc) (`TRA-23`) and PR [trace-watai/trace#71](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-24-pinned-document-retrieval-tests-9cc9aaf4ae04) (`TRA-24`) are part of live readiness too: a Gemini run against unstable tools or uncontrolled retrieval is only an API smoke test, not a TRACE-ready evaluation. ## Objective Define the readiness criteria for live Gemini and future non-fixture evaluation runs. Justin should give the team a clear decision framework for when a live run is TRACE-ready, demo-ready, or still just experimental. ## Scope Cover the full chain from provider call to downstream artifacts: * Model adapter behavior and normalized `AgentAction` output. * Transcript shape and provider metadata. * Tool-call normalization, final answer normalization, error handling, timeout behavior, and retry boundaries. * Controlled support environment behavior, deterministic tool side effects, and inspectable final state. * Controlled retrieval behavior, stable provenance, and ranking overrides needed for reproducible failures. * `model_response` trace event expectations and what raw provider payload may safely contain. * Offline test policy versus manual live smoke test policy. * Required run artifacts for verifier, attribution, failure bundle, regression, and dashboard consumers. * What needs to be true before a Gemini run can be used in the demo narrative or readiness review. ## Deliverables * Live-agent readiness checklist with three levels: * Experimental: allowed for local exploration only. * Integration-ready: produces artifacts downstream systems can consume. * Demo-ready: stable enough to show as part of the TRACE product story. * Merge/readiness questions for Rupert, Samrath, Karan, Darrel, Skye, Evan Yang, and Emily. * Recommended manual smoke command and expected artifacts for a real Gemini run. * Risk note for API keys, rate limits, provider timeouts, raw payload redaction, non-determinism, environment nondeterminism, retrieval ranking drift, and no-network CI rules. * Decision recommendation on whether the first demo should remain fixture-first, live-model optional, or live-model required. ## Acceptance Criteria * Criteria distinguish "provider call works" from "TRACE evaluation works." * Every readiness criterion maps to a downstream consumer or product claim. * The checklist explicitly preserves offline tests and fixture determinism. * The checklist names the minimal manual live validation needed before demo use. * The checklist accounts for PR [trace-watai/trace#64](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/implement-geminimodeladapternext-action-first-live-provider-c0f67230ba5a), PR [trace-watai/trace#70](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-23-mock-environment-validation-and-unit-tests-7dfe53cb1cbc), PR [trace-watai/trace#71](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-24-pinned-document-retrieval-tests-9cc9aaf4ae04), PR [trace-watai/trace#79](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-20-run-data-read-apis-via-a-typed-runreader-service-23192dfc8fdd), and PR [trace-watai/trace#80](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-54-recovery-re-land-orphaned-run-index-pr-56-75f450b-ad22c0819d22) because live runs depend on model behavior, environment/RAG behavior, and artifact-read behavior together. * Open questions are assigned to the correct owners and linked back to Linear issues. * Katharine has at least one technical audit follow-up if code-level contract validation is needed. ## Suggested Technical Approach Start from these current sources: * `src/trace_harness/models/gemini.py` on `main` still scaffolds `next_action` as `NotImplementedError`. * PR [trace-watai/trace#64](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/implement-geminimodeladapternext-action-first-live-provider-c0f67230ba5a) implements native Gemini function calling, `model_response` events, and offline adapter tests. * PR [trace-watai/trace#70](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-23-mock-environment-validation-and-unit-tests-7dfe53cb1cbc) validates mock environment/tool/state/retrieval behavior. * PR [trace-watai/trace#71](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-24-pinned-document-retrieval-tests-9cc9aaf4ae04) adds pinned retrieval/ranking controls. * PR [trace-watai/trace#79](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-20-run-data-read-apis-via-a-typed-runreader-service-23192dfc8fdd) and PR [trace-watai/trace#80](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-54-recovery-re-land-orphaned-run-index-pr-56-75f450b-ad22c0819d22) define the read path and run-index recovery that live runs will need for dashboard/evaluation consumers. * `src/trace_harness/runner/agent_runner.py` owns the step loop and event emission. * `docs/modules.md` defines no-network tests, schema versioning, and runner/model boundaries. * `docs/trace_schema.md` reserves `model_response` for real provider output. ## Collaborators / Reviewers * Rupert: Gemini adapter and runner behavior. * Samrath: trace/artifact contract and model_response event shape. * Evan Yang: environment/tool/RAG behavior under live model calls. * Emily: task validity and fixture readiness. * Karan and Darrel: enough evidence for verifier and attribution. * Skye: dashboard visibility of live-run artifacts. * Katharine: code-level readiness audit. * Mohammed and Sarp: demo/readiness decision. ## Dependencies No hard blocker for writing criteria. Final validation depends on the Gemini PR and any required environment, retrieval, runner, and read-path PRs landing. ## Handoff / Blocks Feeds `TRA-64`, `TRA-59`, `TRA-41`, `TRA-52`, and demo readiness planning. It should make clear what the team must prove before treating live-agent output as product evidence. ## Out of Scope * Implementing Gemini adapter code. * Choosing long-term provider strategy. * Requiring live API calls in CI. * Expanding beyond the refund/support vertical slice before the first live-readiness path is validated.

TRA-66 Build the live TRACE interface compatibility matrix across code, PRs, and Linear

## Context TRACE's active work is now spread across `main`, open GitHub PRs, and Linear. Several contracts are in motion at the same time: * Task schema and authoring quality: `TRA-6`, PR [trace-watai/trace#75](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-6-task-validity-rubric-validate-fixtures-checker-ca018bad679f). * Mock environment and controlled RAG: `TRA-23`, `TRA-24`, PRs [trace-watai/trace#70](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-23-mock-environment-validation-and-unit-tests-7dfe53cb1cbc) and [trace-watai/trace#71](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-24-pinned-document-retrieval-tests-9cc9aaf4ae04). * Gemini / live-agent behavior: `TRA-59`, PR [trace-watai/trace#64](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/implement-geminimodeladapternext-action-first-live-provider-c0f67230ba5a). * Run artifacts and read APIs: `TRA-20`, `TRA-54`, `TRA-63`, PRs [trace-watai/trace#79](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-20-run-data-read-apis-via-a-typed-runreader-service-23192dfc8fdd) and [trace-watai/trace#80](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-54-recovery-re-land-orphaned-run-index-pr-56-75f450b-ad22c0819d22). * Verifier schema/evidence: `TRA-16`, `TRA-56`, `TRA-57`, PRs [trace-watai/trace#72](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-56-constrain-evidenceitemkind-to-an-evidencekind-enum-963d894f0aa5) and [trace-watai/trace#73](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-56-add-verifierinput-model-evidencekind-enum-update-verifier-c97f7dd13f53). * Frontend data contracts: `TRA-27`, `TRA-60`, `TRA-61`, `TRA-62`, PR [#78](). * Failure-card/repair contracts: `TRA-11`, PR [trace-watai/trace#74](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-11-define-canonical-failure-card-and-repair-package-schemas-ce92b466a9dd). * Attribution semantics: `TRA-13` done, `TRA-10` still to integrate into judge work. The project needs one compatibility matrix that says what is true on `main`, what is pending in PRs, what Linear says, and what consumers need before the next end-to-end demo. ## Objective Create the live interface compatibility matrix Justin will use to coordinate stream owners and prevent schema drift. ## Scope Review the following contracts: * `TaskSpec`, task-validity rubric, fixtures, and `validate-fixtures` behavior. * Environment state, tool schemas, side-effect semantics, and retrieval/ranking controls. * Gemini/live-provider adapter output, normalized `AgentAction`, and `model_response` trace behavior. * `TraceEvent` and event types, including future frontend trace-event contract. * Run artifact layout, run index, run listing, and read path. * `VerifierResult`, `FailedCheck`, `EvidenceItem`, evidence kind vocabulary, severity, and release-blocking fields. * `AttributionResult` and diagnostic step semantics. * `FailureCard`, `RepairPackage`, and `RegressionArtifact` fields. * Dashboard TypeScript mirrors and parse helpers. ## Deliverables * Compatibility matrix with columns: ```text Contract | Producer | Consumer(s) | Current main version | Pending PR/issue | Consumer gap | Reviewer | Required validation | Risk level | Next action ``` * Short written summary of the top integration risks and who needs to talk. * A list of stale or misleading Linear parent issues whose status/description should be updated after PRs land. * Recommended review order for active PRs if merge sequencing matters. ## Acceptance Criteria * Matrix references concrete files, PRs, and Linear IDs rather than generic component names. * It clearly distinguishes `main` state from open-PR state. * Each P0/P1 contract has a producer, consumer, and reviewer named. * The matrix covers the full open PR stack: [trace-watai/trace#64](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/implement-geminimodeladapternext-action-first-live-provider-c0f67230ba5a), [trace-watai/trace#70](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-23-mock-environment-validation-and-unit-tests-7dfe53cb1cbc), [trace-watai/trace#71](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-24-pinned-document-retrieval-tests-9cc9aaf4ae04), [trace-watai/trace#72](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-56-constrain-evidenceitemkind-to-an-evidencekind-enum-963d894f0aa5), [trace-watai/trace#73](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-56-add-verifierinput-model-evidencekind-enum-update-verifier-c97f7dd13f53), [trace-watai/trace#74](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-11-define-canonical-failure-card-and-repair-package-schemas-ce92b466a9dd), [trace-watai/trace#75](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-6-task-validity-rubric-validate-fixtures-checker-ca018bad679f), [#78](), [trace-watai/trace#79](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-20-run-data-read-apis-via-a-typed-runreader-service-23192dfc8fdd), and [trace-watai/trace#80](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-54-recovery-re-land-orphaned-run-index-pr-56-75f450b-ad22c0819d22). * At least one dashboard-facing validation path is identified for every artifact the UI will render. * At least one code-level follow-up is delegated to Katharine with exact files/PRs to inspect. * Mohammed and Sarp can use the output to understand what is actually blocking readiness. ## Suggested Technical Approach Start with: * `README.md`, `docs/modules.md`, `docs/first_vertical_slice.md`, `docs/future_api.md`, `docs/future_dashboard.md`. * `src/trace_harness/tasks/`, `src/trace_harness/environment/`, `src/trace_harness/models/`, `src/trace_harness/runner/`, `src/trace_harness/tracing/`, `src/trace_harness/verifiers/`, `src/trace_harness/attribution/`, `src/trace_harness/failure_bundles/`, `src/trace_harness/regression/`. * `apps/dashboard/src/types/`. * Open PRs [trace-watai/trace#64](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/implement-geminimodeladapternext-action-first-live-provider-c0f67230ba5a), [trace-watai/trace#70](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-23-mock-environment-validation-and-unit-tests-7dfe53cb1cbc), [trace-watai/trace#71](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-24-pinned-document-retrieval-tests-9cc9aaf4ae04), [trace-watai/trace#72](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-56-constrain-evidenceitemkind-to-an-evidencekind-enum-963d894f0aa5), [trace-watai/trace#73](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-56-add-verifierinput-model-evidencekind-enum-update-verifier-c97f7dd13f53), [trace-watai/trace#74](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-11-define-canonical-failure-card-and-repair-package-schemas-ce92b466a9dd), [trace-watai/trace#75](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-6-task-validity-rubric-validate-fixtures-checker-ca018bad679f), [#78](), [trace-watai/trace#79](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-20-run-data-read-apis-via-a-typed-runreader-service-23192dfc8fdd), and [trace-watai/trace#80](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-54-recovery-re-land-orphaned-run-index-pr-56-75f450b-ad22c0819d22) and their linked Linear issues. ## Collaborators / Reviewers * Katharine for code-level verification and embedded follow-ups. * Emily for task validity and fixtures. * Evan Yang for mock environment and retrieval behavior. * Rupert for runner/live-agent behavior. * Samrath for run artifacts/read APIs. * Skye for dashboard contracts. * Karan for verifier schema. * Darrel for attribution semantics. * Samir for failure bundles and regression artifacts. * Mohammed and Sarp for project-level implications. ## Dependencies No hard blocker. This should be done from the current live workspace and updated as PRs land. ## Handoff / Blocks Feeds `TRA-64` and tells Katharine where to embed first. It should also inform `TRA-47`, `TRA-52`, and eventual readiness review work. ## Out of Scope * Implementing the fixes directly. * Rewriting schemas without stream-owner agreement. * Creating a new architecture document that duplicates existing docs instead of updating the live matrix.

TRA-59 Implement GeminiModelAdapter

**Context** The runner, environment, and trace pipeline all work today, but the only agent "brain" is the deterministic fixture script. `GeminiModelAdapter.next_action` is currently a scaffold that raises `NotImplementedError` ([src/trace_harness/models/gemini.py]()). Implementing the reviewed implementation turns the scripted skeleton into a real agent — the first time a live model actually drives the refund/support loop. the reviewed implementation is the top "Build next" item for the `models/` module. **Objective** Implement `GeminiModelAdapter.next_action` so a real Gemini model can drive a task end-to-end, returning the same normalized `AgentAction` the fixture adapter produces — so the runner, verifiers, attribution, and dashboard never know which provider produced a trace. **Scope** Transcript → Gemini contents conversion; `ToolSpec` → Gemini function declarations (native tool mode); the `google-genai` SDK call; response normalization into one `AgentAction` (tool call or final answer) with the raw payload stashed in `AgentAction.raw`; SDK-error mapping to `ModelAdapterError`. Native function-calling first; JSON tool-mode fallback can follow. **Deliverables** * `next_action` implemented per the 5-step design in the module docstring. * `_transcript_to_contents(transcript)` and `_tools_to_declarations(tools)` helpers (reading structured `tool_call` data from `Message.metadata`, per [TRA-58](https://linear.app/trace-watai/issue/TRA-58/agent-skeleton-fixes-transcript-shape-model-call-timeout-cli-provider) — not re-parsing JSON from `content`). * Response → `AgentAction` normalization, with `raw` populated. * SDK errors mapped to `ModelAdapterError` so the runner records a clean `model_error` termination. * Offline unit tests using a **recorded** SDK response (no live API call). * A `model_response` trace event emitted by the runner so the raw payload lands in the trace (today the runner records `model_action` with `raw` excluded and never emits `model_response`). * README/.env note for obtaining a free Gemini key. **Acceptance Criteria** * A task runs end-to-end with `--provider gemini` (CLI plumbing landed in [TRA-58](https://linear.app/trace-watai/issue/TRA-58/agent-skeleton-fixes-transcript-shape-model-call-timeout-cli-provider)). * Tool calls and final answers are normalized identically to the fixture adapter (same downstream artifacts). * SDK failures terminate the run as `model_error`, not an uncaught crash. * **Tests stay fully offline** — no test requires an API key or network (repo-wide constraint). * The trace records the provider's raw response payload. **Suggested Technical Approach** Start with `tool_mode = native` function calling. Build the transcript→contents converter against a recorded response and unit-test the normalization in isolation before any live call. Keep the live request behind a per-call request timeout so a hung call is bounded at the provider level (complements the runner-level daemon-thread timeout from [TRA-58](https://linear.app/trace-watai/issue/TRA-58/agent-skeleton-fixes-transcript-shape-model-call-timeout-cli-provider)). **Collaborators / Reviewers** Samrath (trace — `model_response` event + raw payload shape), Karan Gupta / Darrel Wihandi (confirm real-model traces carry enough for verification/attribution, especially when `reasoning` is absent). **Dependencies** Runner skeleton ([TRA-7](https://linear.app/trace-watai/issue/TRA-7/build-baseline-target-agent-runner-skeleton)), CLI provider flag + model-call timeout ([TRA-58](https://linear.app/trace-watai/issue/TRA-58/agent-skeleton-fixes-transcript-shape-model-call-timeout-cli-provider)), the `models/base.py` contract types, and `google-genai` (already an optional `[gemini]` dependency). **Handoff / Blocks** Unblocks real (non-scripted) agent runs, multi-run orchestration with non-deterministic models, and any evaluation that needs live-model behavior. **Out of Scope** Retries / backoff, rate limiting, cost tracking, parallel tool calls, streaming, and providers other than Gemini.

TRA-67 Define live-agent readiness criteria for Gemini and non-fixture evaluation runs

## Context TRACE's `main` branch has a deterministic fixture-based vertical slice. That is valuable, but the next product question is when a real model/provider run should count as a meaningful TRACE evaluation instead of just an API smoke test. Open Gemini work (`TRA-59`, PR [trace-watai/trace#64](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/implement-geminimodeladapternext-action-first-live-provider-c0f67230ba5a)) is the first live-adapter step. the reviewed implementation depends on runner transcript shape, model-call timeout, provider flags, trace events, environment behavior, verifier readiness, artifact read paths, and controlled RAG/tool behavior. Without explicit readiness criteria, the team can easily merge a live adapter that technically calls a model but does not produce enough evidence for verification, attribution, failure bundles, regression, or dashboard replay. PR [trace-watai/trace#70](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-23-mock-environment-validation-and-unit-tests-7dfe53cb1cbc) (`TRA-23`) and PR [trace-watai/trace#71](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-24-pinned-document-retrieval-tests-9cc9aaf4ae04) (`TRA-24`) are part of live readiness too: a Gemini run against unstable tools or uncontrolled retrieval is only an API smoke test, not a TRACE-ready evaluation. ## Objective Define the readiness criteria for live Gemini and future non-fixture evaluation runs. Justin should give the team a clear decision framework for when a live run is TRACE-ready, demo-ready, or still just experimental. ## Scope Cover the full chain from provider call to downstream artifacts: * Model adapter behavior and normalized `AgentAction` output. * Transcript shape and provider metadata. * Tool-call normalization, final answer normalization, error handling, timeout behavior, and retry boundaries. * Controlled support environment behavior, deterministic tool side effects, and inspectable final state. * Controlled retrieval behavior, stable provenance, and ranking overrides needed for reproducible failures. * `model_response` trace event expectations and what raw provider payload may safely contain. * Offline test policy versus manual live smoke test policy. * Required run artifacts for verifier, attribution, failure bundle, regression, and dashboard consumers. * What needs to be true before a Gemini run can be used in the demo narrative or readiness review. ## Deliverables * Live-agent readiness checklist with three levels: * Experimental: allowed for local exploration only. * Integration-ready: produces artifacts downstream systems can consume. * Demo-ready: stable enough to show as part of the TRACE product story. * Merge/readiness questions for Rupert, Samrath, Karan, Darrel, Skye, Evan Yang, and Emily. * Recommended manual smoke command and expected artifacts for a real Gemini run. * Risk note for API keys, rate limits, provider timeouts, raw payload redaction, non-determinism, environment nondeterminism, retrieval ranking drift, and no-network CI rules. * Decision recommendation on whether the first demo should remain fixture-first, live-model optional, or live-model required. ## Suggested Technical Approach Start from these current sources: * `src/trace_harness/models/gemini.py` on `main` still scaffolds `next_action` as `NotImplementedError`. * PR [trace-watai/trace#64](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/implement-geminimodeladapternext-action-first-live-provider-c0f67230ba5a) implements native Gemini function calling, `model_response` events, and offline adapter tests. * PR [trace-watai/trace#70](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-23-mock-environment-validation-and-unit-tests-7dfe53cb1cbc) validates mock environment/tool/state/retrieval behavior. * PR [trace-watai/trace#71](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-24-pinned-document-retrieval-tests-9cc9aaf4ae04) adds pinned retrieval/ranking controls. * PR [trace-watai/trace#79](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-20-run-data-read-apis-via-a-typed-runreader-service-23192dfc8fdd) and PR [trace-watai/trace#80](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-54-recovery-re-land-orphaned-run-index-pr-56-75f450b-ad22c0819d22) define the read path and run-index recovery that live runs will need for dashboard/evaluation consumers. * `src/trace_harness/runner/agent_runner.py` owns the step loop and event emission. * `docs/modules.md` defines no-network tests, schema versioning, and runner/model boundaries. * `docs/trace_schema.md` reserves `model_response` for real provider output. ## Acceptance Criteria * Matrix references concrete files, PRs, and Linear IDs rather than generic component names. * the reviewed implementation clearly distinguishes `main` state from open-PR state. * Each P0/P1 contract has a producer, consumer, and reviewer named. * The matrix covers the full open PR stack: [trace-watai/trace#64](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/implement-geminimodeladapternext-action-first-live-provider-c0f67230ba5a), [trace-watai/trace#70](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-23-mock-environment-validation-and-unit-tests-7dfe53cb1cbc), [trace-watai/trace#71](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-24-pinned-document-retrieval-tests-9cc9aaf4ae04), [trace-watai/trace#72](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-56-constrain-evidenceitemkind-to-an-evidencekind-enum-963d894f0aa5), [trace-watai/trace#73](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-56-add-verifierinput-model-evidencekind-enum-update-verifier-c97f7dd13f53), [trace-watai/trace#74](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-11-define-canonical-failure-card-and-repair-package-schemas-ce92b466a9dd), [trace-watai/trace#75](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-6-task-validity-rubric-validate-fixtures-checker-ca018bad679f), [#78](), [trace-watai/trace#79](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-20-run-data-read-apis-via-a-typed-runreader-service-23192dfc8fdd), and [trace-watai/trace#80](https://linear.app/trace-watai/review/tra-54-recovery-re-land-orphaned-run-index-pr-56-75f450b-ad22c0819d22). * At least one dashboard-facing validation path is identified for every artifact the UI will render. * At least one code-level follow-up is delegated to Katharine with exact files/PRs to inspect. * Mohammed and Sarp can use the output to understand what is actually blocking readiness. ##
